stem: The region bounded by the line \( y = x \), the \( x \)-axis, and the vertical lines \( x = [[a]] \) and \( x = [[b]] \) is revolved about the line \( y = -[[L]] \). Find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: Distances are measured from the line \( y=-[[L]] \): the outer radius is \( x+[[L]] \) (from \( y=x \)) and the inner radius is \( [[L]] \) (from \( y=0 \)). The volume is \( \pi\int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\left((x+[[L]])^{2}-[[L]]^{2}\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This revolves about the \( x \)-axis, ignoring the shift to \( y=-[[L]] \).
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This forgets the inner radius (the gap between the axis and the line \( y=-[[L]] \)).
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The region bounded by the line \( x = y \), the \( y \)-axis, and the horizontal lines \( y = [[a]] \) and \( y = [[b]] \) is revolved about the vertical line \( x = -[[L]] \). Find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: Because the axis of revolution is vertical, we slice with horizontal washers. Distances are measured from the line \( x=-[[L]] \): the outer radius is \( y+[[L]] \) (from \( x=y \)) and the inner radius is \( [[L]] \) (from \( x=0 \)). The volume is \( \pi\int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\left((y+[[L]])^{2}-[[L]]^{2}\right)dy = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This revolves about the \( y \)-axis, ignoring the shift to \( x=-[[L]] \).
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This forgets the inner radius (the gap between the axis and the line \( x=-[[L]] \)).
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The region between the lines \( y = x + [[p]] \) and \( y = x - [[q]] \), for \( [[a]] \le x \le [[b]] \), is revolved about the horizontal line \( y = -[[L]] \), which lies below the entire region. Find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: Since neither boundary touches the axis \( y=-[[L]] \), BOTH radii are shifted by \( [[L]] \): the outer radius is \( (x+[[p]])+[[L]] \) and the inner radius is \( (x-[[q]])+[[L]] \). The volume is \( \pi\int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\left(((x+[[p]])+[[L]])^{2}-((x-[[q]])+[[L]])^{2}\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This treats the washer as a constant-radius ring times the width, ignoring how the radii vary with \( x \).
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This forgets to shift either radius by \( [[L]] \), as if revolving about the \( x \)-axis.
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The region bounded by the line \( y = [[k]]x \), the \( x \)-axis, and the vertical lines \( x = [[a]] \) and \( x = [[b]] \) is revolved about the vertical line \( x = -[[L]] \). Using cylindrical shells, find the volume of the solid generated.
solution: Each shell at position \( x \) has radius (distance to the axis \( x=-[[L]] \)) equal to \( x+[[L]] \) and height \( [[k]]x \). The volume is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]} 2\pi(x+[[L]])([[k]]x)\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the shell radius \( x \) as if revolving about the \( y \)-axis, ignoring the shift to \( x=-[[L]] \).
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This uses the disk/washer method instead of shells.
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( 2\pi \) from the correct volume.
